prompt_inputs.goal: High-performance fuel for marathon training day (long run scheduled)

solution_criteria: Meal plan must be strictly vegan, gluten-free, and entirely free of all nuts (including peanuts).
Total estimated caloric intake must be adequate for a high-performance athlete on a marathon training day (e.g., 2800-3500 kcal, depending on intensity/duration).
Macronutrient distribution should support athletic performance (e.g., high carbohydrates for sustained energy, adequate protein for recovery, healthy fats).
Plan must provide 3 main meals and 1-2 snacks, clearly outlining food items and suggested portion sizes for a single day.
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Test with an athlete requiring a very specific, high-performance diet (e.g., marathon runner) combined with multiple, strict dietary restrictions (e.g., vegan, gluten-free, nut allergy) to ensure nutritional adequacy and adherence.

solution_criteria: The meal plan must clearly define at least 3 main meals (Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner) and may include 1-2 snacks for a single day.
Meals should primarily consist of common, readily available ingredients and simple preparation methods suitable for an average home cook.

Each main meal should qualitatively demonstrate a balance of macronutrients (e.g., protein source, complex carbohydrate, healthy fat) appropriate for an athlete's general fitness and maintenance goals.
The entire 1-day meal plan should be presented in a concise and compact format, without excessive descriptive text.
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Evaluate a common use case with an average height/weight athlete (e.g., 175cm, 70kg) aiming for general fitness and weight maintenance with no dietary restrictions, expecting a balanced, easily achievable meal plan.

prompt_inputs.goal: Rapid weight cut for a competition (approx. 1-1.5kg/week loss)

solution_criteria: The total caloric intake for the day must reflect a significant deficit appropriate for rapid weight loss for a 190cm, 110kg athlete (e.g., 2000-2500 kcal).
The meal plan must prioritize high protein intake (e.g., >180g) to support muscle preservation during rapid weight loss.
No red meat (beef, pork, lamb, venison, etc.) should be included in any meal.

The plan should provide a balanced intake of macronutrients (excluding the specific restriction and goal-driven adjustments) and micronutrients from acceptable sources, presented concisely as a 1-day plan.
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Challenge the prompt with an extreme body composition goal (e.g., significant weight cut for a competition or rapid muscle gain) for a large athlete (e.g., 190cm, 110kg) with a single, common restriction like 'no red meat' or 'dairy-free', to check for appropriate calorie and macronutrient adjustments.
